reasoning: The community prediction on May 29 was 50%. Since then, 9 more days have elapsed. The total period is roughly 36 days (May 20 to June 30, excluding Sundays). By May 29, 8 days had passed. If the community thought the probability of a 'yes' resolution in the remaining 28 days was ~50%, the daily probability of a 'yes' (both papers omitting the word 'tariff') is around 2.4%. With roughly 19 days remaining from June 8 to June 30, the probability of it occurring in the future is 1 - (1 - 0.024)^19 ≈ 37%. It's possible the event has already occurred since May 29 and the question hasn't updated/resolved yet, but lacking any observed evidence of it (such as a Metaculus comment), I will treat the remaining time as the primary driver of probability and adjust the 50% base rate down to account for the passage of time. Tariffs remain a dominant ongoing news story in the current geopolitical and economic climate.
